Regulator_Type.reason: jai1 is explicitly described as a mutant for COI1, the JA receptor component.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Results show jai1 regulates SGA metabolism via JA signalling.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: The paper shows downstream effects on SSR2, GAME11, GAME4 and GAME12 and mentions JAZs generally in the JA pathway, but does not frame these all as direct primary targets of COI1/JAI1 in the Results.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Results and proteomics show JAI1 positively mediates accumulation/expression of SGA biosynthetic components, indirectly through JA signalling.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: COI1/JAI1 functions in JA-Ile perception, and MeJA treatment is used throughout.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Fig. S4 and Results state jai1 has significantly lower basal α-tomatine and reduced abundance of SGA biosynthetic proteins.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: The paper concludes JAI1 regulates SGA metabolism by activating SGA biosynthetic enzymes.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: jai1 mutant analysis, qRT-PCR, proteomics and LC-MS/metabolite profiling are all described.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three listed findings are stated in the Results for jai1/Fig. S4.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper cites previous work showing JAZ2, JAZ6 and JAZ7 interact with JAI1 in tomato.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The study broadly frames JA perception as tied to defence metabolism; reduced JA perception lowering defence metabolism is supported conceptually by the data.
